content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

May 21, 1997


Jack McGrath
5318 Agnes Avenue
Valley Village, CA 91607

Re:    Your Request for Informal Assistance
       Our File No. I-97-228

Dear Mr. McGrath:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹ You have not indicated whether you are an officeholder, candidate or
committee as defined in the Act. Therefore, we are providing this advice as informal assistance
pursuant to regulations 18329,² and our advice is based on the assumption that you are not an
officeholder, candidate or existing committee.

QUESTION

     Are you required to file a statement of organization if you market to the general public
campaign mementos such as T-shirts, hats and pins. You will use an 800 toll free number, take
orders, and send merchandise. You will not solicit or raise donations, and will not support or
oppose any candidate.

CONCLUSION

     Under the circumstances you have described, you are not required to file a statement of
organization or other campaign reports under the Act.

ANALYSIS

     The Act requires disclosure of contributions received and expenditures made in election

___________________________

¹Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18100-18995
of the California Code of Regulations.

²Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Jack McGrath
Our File No. I-97-228
Page 2

campaigns. To accomplish this, the Act requires reporting by state and local elected
officeholders, candidates for state and local elective offices, and committees.

"'Committee' means any person or combination of persons
who directly or indirectly does any of the following:
(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand dollars
($1,000) or more in a calendar year.
(b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year; or
(c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars
($10,000) or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of
candidates or committees...."

Section 82013.

The terms "contribution" and "expenditure" are defined in the Act to mean payments
made for political purposes, i.e., payments which are made for the purpose of influencing the
action of the voters to vote for or against a particular candidate or ballot measure. (Sections
82015 and 82025; Regulations 18215 and 18225.) In circumstances where full and adequate
consideration is received in exchange for a particular payment, no contribution exists under the
Act. (Section 82015.)

Therefore, if you are merely selling merchandise to the public for its fair market value
and are not receiving contributions or making expenditures for the purpose of supporting or
opposing state or local candidates or measures, you are not required to register as a committee or
file campaign disclosure reports in connection with this activity.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[Signature: Carla Wardlow]

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
